title: pipeline.regimes path inference prefers stale 5y D1_22y stub for GBPUSD (and crashes on 0-labeled sanity)
why: data/D1_22y/GBPUSD_D1.csv is a 1310-bar 5y stub, not a 22y archive; the CLI default inference picks it over the 27y data/D1 file, producing a 0-labeled parquet and a ZeroDivisionError in sanity_report. Workaround used: explicit --data-path data/D1/GBPUSD_D1.csv. Also sanity_report should not divide by zero on 0 labeled bars (SOLUSD H4 hits the same).
scope: inference: prefer the file with MORE bars (or FX always data/D1, matching run_proposal logic)
sanity_report: guard total==0 with an explicit insufficient-history message
optionally delete the misleading GBPUSD stub from D1_22y
recommendation: Fix before the 21-jun pre-live data refresh so the one-command refresh is safe.

history.reason: All 3 scope items: (1) _resolve_data_path prefers the file with MORE bars when both data/D1_22y and data/D1 candidates exist (ties -> primary, preserving the metals 22y preference); (2) sanity_report returns an explicit insufficient_history verdict on 0 labeled bars instead of ZeroDivisionError, and the CLI exits 1 so refresh scripts fail loudly; (3) the misleading 1310-bar GBPUSD_D1.csv stub deleted from data/D1_22y (untracked). Verified end-to-end: GBPUSD D1 inference now picks data/D1 (7794 bars, 6471 labeled). 7 regression tests in tests/test_regimes_b0152.py.
